element_introduction_order.mastery_context: First game teaches directional movement and passing mechanics
Voice callout ('Get the pass'/'It's a pass') reinforces successful execution
Three consecutive bumps without hitting another player trigger fight initiation

element_introduction_order.first_appears: After third consecutive bump or aggressive play
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: High/low punch and block system; health bar of 5 hits; loser receives 2-minute penalty
5-on-4 skaters creates scoring opportunity; up to 4 players can be penalized (5-on-1 max)

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Fight occurs near net; or after reaching overtime
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Referee breaks up fight; shooter stands on blue line (soccer-style); tiebreaker mechanic
Gradius-style spaceship game; optional content; serves as break between match segments
Playoff-style bracket; must beat all opposing teams to win Cup
Three difficulty levels available upfront; Pro is most difficult

typical_relief_triggers: Opponent penalized and sent to penalty box
typical_tension_triggers: Tied score approaching end of game
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Fast-paced arcade hockey with rhythmic tension cycles around physical contact. Bumping creates anticipatory tension leading to fighting sequences. Each fight resolves with clear win/loss outcome and penalty assignment. Power play advantage creates offensive pressure relief. Intermission provides narrative break. Playoff tournament mode sustains long-term progression tension.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Intermission minigame appears optionally after second intermission; playable by Player One or Konami advertisements shown

difficulty_curve.early_game: Junior difficulty available for learning skating, passing, and basic collision mechanics without high fight frequency
difficulty_curve.mid_game: College difficulty introduces more aggressive AI; increased fighting encounters and penalty management
difficulty_curve.late_game: Pro difficulty features aggressive opponents, frequent fights, and penalty stacking (up to 5-on-1 scenarios)
notable_spikes: First intentional bump encounter and fight initiation learning curve
Transition from Exhibition to Tournament mode (persistent opponent progression)
Penalty shot introduction (distinct minigame rules departure)
Final tournament match (cumulative difficulty from bracket progression)

notable_level_patterns.description: Standard 3-period ice hockey match with faceoff opening, continuous play with pause for intermission, final period leading to end-game resolution (win, loss, or overtime)
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Exhibition match or any Tournament bracket match

notable_level_patterns.description: Three consecutive bumps without other player contact → pre-fight confrontation → optional punch exchange → full fight minigame with 5-hit health bars → winner continues play, loser penalized 2 minutes
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any match where aggressive play is pursued

notable_level_patterns.description: Opponent penalized by fight loss → numerical advantage (5-on-4 minimum) → attacking team has offensive pressure window to score while opponent is short-handed
Fight erupts in front of net (blue line area) → referee stops play and awards penalty shot → shooting player stands on blue line → 1-on-1 against goaltender in simplified mechanic
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Matches with net-adjacent fights or overtime tiebreaker

notable_level_patterns.description: Team selection → sequential matches against 7 other teams in playoff elimination style → Cup awarded to final winner
